notes: The game seems to run perfectly after the following trick to prevent Uplay client from updating (you have to run the game once in order to create the WINE prefix): 1) Go to (your_steam_library_folder)/steamapps/compatdata/354380/pfx/drive_c/Program Files (x86)/Ubisoft/Ubisoft Game Launcher. 2) Open version.txt file and note the version number. 3) Download this Uplay version: https://web.archive.org/web/20180927054350/http://ubi.li/4vxt9. 4) Using winetricks or protontricks, uninstall Uplay and then install the version you've just downloaded ("Select the default wineprefix" > "Run uninstaller"). 5) Run the game.
title: Assassin's Creed® Revelations

notes: Launch the first time, login to uplay, exit. The eagle-eye view generates bright fog that doesn't disappear when you quit eagle-eye view. Without this glitch, it will be perfectly playable.
title: Assassin's Creed® Revelations

notes: Game opens and runs perfectly. I did not mention any problem, lack or stutter.
title: Assassin's Creed® Revelations

notes: Low framerates, minor graphical problems. DON"T use wine D3D. It make everything worse. Well, the framerate was better, but everything else was worse (and blindingly bright).
title: Assassin's Creed® Revelations

notes: Runs at a lower framerate than it would on Windows given the hardware, but it works perfectly with no glitches.
title: Assassin's Creed® Revelations

notes:
Launched Uplay through stream installer just fine after restarting Steam.

The game ran very poorly compared to Windows 10. It was smooth in the loading screen but then extremely stuttery experience when in game and only managed around 20-30fps regardless of graphical settings used. Very uncomfortable to play.
title: Assassin's Creed® Revelations

notes: Runs perfectly, performance was generally good. Better than I remember under Windows 7 years ago. Controls work, all graphics set to max fine.
title: Assassin's Creed® Revelations

notes: Runs very well without any tweaks. I recommend turning off the Uplay overlay to improve the performance.
